guardrails.description: When skill A dispatches skill B and B's prepare script resolves a flag from CLI + config inputs, every SKILL.md decision site in B that gates on that flag must cite the resolved field from prepare output. Sites that inspect raw $ARGUMENTS or config.X directly after Step 1 (CONSUME) fail this guardrail. Carve-outs for persistent project state (e.g., CI scaffolding) must include an inline rationale comment.
Within a single skill, when two or more SKILL.md sites gate the same behavioral concept, the gate condition must be expressed identically.
Each contractual flag must have exactly one resolution site — typically the prepare script. Downstream consumers must reference the resolved field by name; they must not re-derive the value.

Every SKILL.md decision site that gates on a script-resolved flag must cite flags.X (not config.X or raw $ARGUMENTS) after Step 1 (CONSUME). Persistent project state carve-outs require an inline rationale comment.
Implementations that introduce or retain divergent gate phrasings for the same concept fail this guardrail.
